question_text: A household appliance is labelled with a power rating of 2000 W. What does this power rating tell you about the appliance?
correct_answer: The power rating of 2000 W indicates that the appliance transfers or uses 2000 joules of energy every second when it is operating at full capacity.
explanation: Power is measured in watts (W), and it tells us how much energy an appliance uses per second. A 2000 W appliance uses 2000 joules of energy every second. Higher power ratings mean appliances use energy faster.
